# Questions in Livestream 158

Live Stream #158: Biblically Accurate Harsh Language

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=570" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:30</a> | How could Jesus appear to "the Twelve" in 1 Corinthians 15 if Judas was already dead and Matthias had not yet been chosen? | He says "the Twelve" functioned as a title for the apostolic body rather than a strict headcount. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=690" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:30</a> | Why did Hezekiah use scarab imagery on his seals? | He says ancient iconography was not locked to one religion, so scarab imagery could carry royal or political force without endorsing pagan worship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=831" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:51</a> | How much freedom did an ancient scribe have when writing down dictated material? | He says scribes had some room to work but still operated under genre limits and expectations of faithful transmission. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=1056" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:36</a> | Did Nefertari die before Amun-her-khepeshef, and could that connect to the exodus? | He says it does not appear so: Amun-her-khepeshef died around Ramesses II's year 25, while Nefertari seems to have lived until about year 35. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=1157" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:17</a> | Was Amon king of Judah named after the Egyptian god Amun? | He says no; the Hebrew and Egyptian forms do not transliterate that way, and the Hebrew name has a Semitic meaning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=1420" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:40</a> | Is "you shall not lie" a commandment, or is false witness about malicious deception? | He says false witness is mainly legal or compelled testimony and argues moral situations can require following a higher good. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=1888" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:28</a> | Was Egypt the breadbasket of the Greco-Roman era, and how could Egypt give away so much food when food was often scarce? | He says yes, and points to redirected agriculture, Nile-flood bumper crops, taxation, and shifts from textile crops to food crops. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=2311" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:31</a> | May Christians use the holy anointing oil from Exodus 30? | He says the ceremonial restriction is completed in Christ, but recreating the exact mixture is uncertain because the ingredients are disputed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=2758" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:58</a> | Was he able to identify the cartouche from Tuesday? | He says one cartouche may be Sobekhotep II and the other is still uncertain, but they are not Djoser's. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=2889" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:09</a> | Why should Strong's Concordance be burned? | He says it is badly outdated, gives flattened concordant meanings, and should not replace modern lexicons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=3230" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:50</a> | Did he reach out to Gavin Ortlund about the Josh Bowen matter? | He says no, he has no contact with Gavin Ortlund, and suspects Gavin may have accepted Bowen's personal narrative while noting he does not know for sure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=3662" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:02</a> | What are his thoughts on Tantra? | He says he is not sure what the question means and asks for clarification before answering. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=3691" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:31</a> | What was the Bowen "sob story"? | He describes it as a personal hardship narrative that he thinks is used to deflect criticism rather than answer substantive objections. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=4005" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:45</a> | When can people expect his book on the plagues of Egypt? | He says it is still at the proposal stage, not yet sent to a publisher, and would likely be at least one to two years away if accepted. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=4088" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:08</a> | Who in Egypt would have known of the plagues and exodus, would they have been remembered by Ramesses III, and does the biblical text exaggerate their severity? | He says Lower Egypt around Pi-Ramesses would know most directly, later civil wars could overshadow the events by Ramesses III's time, and he treats the plagues as serious but geographically focused. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=4513" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:13</a> | In the story of Ziusudra, what is the context for the flood line about giving a man back? | He says he has not read that Mesopotamian flood story closely enough and would need to look it up. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=4626" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:06</a> | Is Jesus turning water into wine meant as a callback to Moses turning water into blood? | He allows some thematic resonance but does not treat it as a simple one-to-one symbolic equation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=5275" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:55</a> | Who folds water like a cloth in the Bible? | He says the figure was not biblical but the Egyptian sage Dedi in Papyrus Westcar. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=5652" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:12</a> | Does Matthew 24:15-22 warn observant Jews specifically, not all New Testament believers? | He says it is especially directed to Sabbath-observant Jews, but Christians near Jerusalem also historically heeded the warning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=5991" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:51</a> | Will he go outside to observe the aurora borealis? | He says probably not, though they might look outside after the livestream if viewing conditions allow. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=6315" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:15</a> | Are there good books about historical foods of Egypt? | He recommends Lisa Manniche's *An Ancient Egyptian Herbal* as the closest useful reference, though not strictly a recipe book. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=6488" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:08</a> | What are his thoughts on the Immaculate Conception? | He rejects the Roman Catholic dogma, arguing it is late, unnecessary, and creates problems if extended backward through Mary's ancestry. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=7004" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:56:44</a> | Is there a relationship between Aaron and the Islamic crescent moon and star? | He says not really, and treats the crescent-and-star iconography as having a different historical background. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=7240" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:40</a> | If incense in ancient Egypt is exaggerated, is it true that Ramesses II named several daughters great wives? | He says at least Bintanath was given the role of great queen for ritual purposes after Nefertari's death, not as an ordinary wife. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=7745" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:05</a> | What kind of education would Moses have received? | He says Moses would have been highly educated because he was raised inside the household of Egypt's highest governing class. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=8140" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:40</a> | What is the psychology of secular Bible critics? | He says academic skepticism encourages questioning everything and can become an assumption that biblical books oppose one another and lack a unified canon. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=8649" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:09</a> | What does he think of comparing Genesis 2 to the Egyptian washing-of-the-mouth ritual? | He knows the Egyptian priestly washing-of-the-mouth practice but says he does not see enough context to explain the proposed Genesis 2 connection. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=8840" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:27:20</a> | Have Christian scholars been increasing or decreasing? | He says no one keeps exact numbers; graduates may be steady or increasing, but positions, funding, and influence are decreasing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=9617" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:17</a> | In his clan interpretation of Genesis, where would he fit the missing generations from Terah to Jacob? | He says gaps could fit between Terah and Abraham, Abraham and Isaac, and possibly Jacob and the twelve sons, which better fits the wider evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=10163" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:23</a> | What does the Egyptian word or element "pa" mean in names and words? | He explains it as a common grammatical element and gives a short language lesson rather than a single fixed English equivalent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=10351" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:31</a> | What is the relationship between Platonism, Kabbalah, and Christianity? | He treats them as distinct traditions and warns against blending occult systems into Christianity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=10687" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:58:07</a> | Did non-Christian Jewish Gnostics exist who thought Yahweh was evil? | He says yes, some Jewish Gnostic groups identified Yahweh or Elohim with the demiurge, though groups varied. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=11004" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:24</a> | Did Jesus ever use extremely harsh curse language? | He says yes, citing the cursed fig tree and Jesus' harsh words against the Pharisees. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=11256" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:07:36</a> | Does he worry about the future of Christianity? | He says no, because he trusts God to preserve the church rather than treating its future as dependent on human control. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=11558" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:38</a> | What voice impressions can he do? | He says he has never cataloged them and they tend to come out spontaneously. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=11591" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:13:11</a> | What does a King James Only ministry even do? | He says they argue the King James Version is the only trustworthy word of God and sometimes subordinate Hebrew and Greek to the English translation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=11919" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:39</a> | What is The Master's Seminary? | He identifies it as John MacArthur's seminary and criticizes it as very fundamentalist and parochial. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=12299" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:24:59</a> | Is there any connection between water and creation literature? | He says yes, primordial waters appear widely in Genesis, Egyptian literature, and Mesopotamian material. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=12460" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:27:40</a> | How does God's omnipresence function? | He argues omnipresence is better handled carefully than as a simple "all-present" attribute, because of pantheism or monism concerns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=12678" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:31:18</a> | Can someone write a book on Leviticus without a ministry degree? | He says yes if the work is good enough, but recommends serious commentaries, journal articles, and intellectual rigor. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=13077" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:37:57</a> | Why do people who speak in tongues often sound the same? | He says many examples sound like gibberish rather than languages, and tongues should edify the church and have interpretation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=13358" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:42:38</a> | How close is the Goa'uld language in Stargate to actual Egyptian? | He says it tried to draw on Egyptian in places, but it is not very close and eventually drifted into its own thing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=13429" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:43:49</a> | Would he ever become a pastor? | He says he has considered it and has pastoral training, but he is not ordained and thinks congregations often resist blunt preaching. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=13832" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:50:32</a> | What does he love most about the Lord? | He says God's grace: unmerited favor for sinners. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=13885" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:51:25</a> | What influenced Gnosticism? | He says Gnosticism arose from a convergence of Egyptian religion and Platonic thought, especially where allegorical interpretation flourished. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=14018" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:53:38</a> | If he is an Egyptologist, why has he not been seen on Ancient Aliens? | He jokes that real Egyptologists do not appear on *Ancient Aliens*, only people with suitably strange hair and empty conjecture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=14109" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:09</a> | What is his opinion of the International Standard Version? | He likes the idea of avoiding traditional renderings but says he has not lived with the translation enough to recommend it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=14509" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:01:49</a> | Should book order matter when creating theology? | He says it matters for biblical and historical theology, but less for systematic or strictly exegetical work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=14831" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:07:11</a> | Does he ever travel down to Seattle? | He says rarely, except sometimes for flights, but he would speak at a Seattle church if invited. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=14912" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:08:32</a> | Should churches use instruments like drum sets and electric guitars in worship? | He says he has no objection, noting that instruments were used in ancient worship and that objections to instruments often shift by era. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=15318" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:15:18</a> | Does Deuteronomy 23:15-16 apply to all slaves? | He says the passage appears to apply broadly to escaped slaves, with no qualification limiting it to one class of slave. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/E9vtdhGehes?t=15643" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:20:43</a> | Is Bigfoot real? | He says no; he thinks the available trace evidence has not supported Bigfoot, though some deep-sea cryptids are more conceivable. |
